body.title: CC.0 marks unavailable before compact, available at session start
body.result: /autocompact writes CC.0.available.false; /context-load restores .true; Granny defers during compact
body.intention: I intend that Granny never dispatches tickets to CC.0 during compaction.
body.description:
When /autocompact fires, CC.0 is still marked available.true, so Granny dispatches tickets into the void — CC is mid-compact and can't process them. Fix: /autocompact writes CC.0.available.false before firing /compact; /context-load writes CC.0.available.true at session start. This gates Granny dispatch on whether CC has actually resumed.

**Affected files:** skills/autocompact/SKILL.md, skills/context-load/SKILL.md (repo canonical), plus ~/.claude/skills/ copies
**Design rules:** external state principle — availability flag lives in ~/.granny/available/ not in memory
**Scope boundary:** In scope: write .false before compact, write .true on resume. Out of scope: full dispatch_ack handshake protocol for tmux-based dispatch (larger refactor).
**Completion criteria:** After /autocompact runs, CC.0.available.false exists; Granny's is_available('CC.0') returns False. After /context-load runs, CC.0.available.true exists; is_available returns True.
